title: Sour Smoky Moroccan Lamb Royal Couscous
description: A citrusy, smoky lamb roast served over fluffy couscous, perfect for a festive Moroccan dinner.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon zest, orange zest, lemon juice, orange juice, ground cloves, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper to form a paste.
Toss the lamb cubes in the spice paste until well coated and let them sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to marinate.
Arrange the marinated lamb on a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, turning halfway, until browned and reaching medium‑rare internal temperature.
While the lamb roasts, bring the chicken broth to a gentle simmer in a saucepan.
Place the couscous in a heat‑proof bowl, pour the hot broth over it, stir once, cover, and let it steam for 5 minutes.
After steaming, fluff the couscous with a fork and season with a pinch of salt if needed.
Transfer the roasted lamb onto the couscous, drizzle with additional citrus juice, and serve hot with optional fresh herbs.
Enjoy each portion as a complete, fragrant Moroccan feast.
